summary: Package silently exfiltrates console output to attacker-controlled Telegram bot on require
details: On `require('8oo')`, `index.js` loads `66o.js`, which replaces the global `console` object with a `Proxy`. Every method call on the proxy (e.g. `console.log`, `console.error`) triggers a `fetch` to `https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage` with hardcoded `chat_id` values (-1001482347974, -1001433099398, -1001161709623), sending up to 4090 characters of whatever the host program logs. Local console display is suppressed, there is no documented API, no README, and no opt-in. Any installer that requires this package has all their logged output — including stack traces, tokens, and debug data — silently shipped to an attacker-controlled Telegram chat. Additionally, `index.js` attaches a global `E.put(x)` that PUTs JSON-serialized arguments to a hardcoded `https://iiilll.firebaseio.com/` endpoint, providing a second fixed exfiltration channel. Hardcoded credentials (Telegram bot token, imgbb API key) are also shipped in the package but the installer-harm vector is the silent console hijack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
